verdicts.note: +2 (fully pole_b) rests on the single pamphlet phrase 'historic tax fairness' — retrospective credit-claiming, not a stated agenda to expand wealth taxes. The same pamphlet passage also touts 'cutting small business taxes' [S1], which cuts against a maximal placement. +1 medium is what the evidence supports.

Basis recycles the taxes evidence (revenue funding schools/childcare). Nothing in the dossier addresses the invest-vs-audit tension directly; this is indirect inference from generic investment framing, so confidence should be low.

Evidence is her DESC housing-first employment plus direction-neutral pamphlet items (gun violence, fentanyl). No stated position on policing, enforcement, or diversion. Professional-formation inference for a legislator is indirect; drop to low.
